banners.escrowUpgradeBody: Escrow upgrade required — the escrow is at v{{userVersion}}, mandatory floor is v{{mandatoryVersion}} (latest v{{currentVersion}}). Diamond flows will revert until upgrade.
banners.unsupportedBody: {{state}} (chainId {{chainId}}). Vaipakam runs as a separate Diamond on each supported network — currently live on: {{liveList}}.
banners.unsupportedPendingPlanned: Mainnet rollout to {{pendingList}} is planned for Phase 1.
banners.unsupportedActionsDisabled: Protocol actions are disabled until switching to a supported network.
banners.preflightChecking: Checking on-chain liquidity for this pair…
banners.preflightNoRoute: 0x can't find a route for this collateral → principal pair at the requested size. Liquidations would fall back to the claim-time full-collateral path. Pick a different collateral asset, or proceed knowing the lender's recovery is the raw collateral instead of principal proceeds.
banners.preflightThinPrefix: 0x routed this pair, but with high price impact
banners.preflightThinSuffix: — above the protocol's {{maxSlippage}}% slippage cap. Liquidations may revert and fall back to the claim-time path. Consider smaller collateral amounts or a more liquid asset.
banners.preflightErrorSuffix: . The on-chain liquidity gate still applies.
banners.preflightLiquid: 0x can route this collateral → principal pair within the protocol's slippage cap. Liquidations should settle without falling back.

discordPage.title: Join the Vaipakam Discord
discordPage.tagline: Chat with the core team, other lenders and borrowers, swap collateral-strategy notes, and get early signal on new deployments. Moderation follows the same non-custodial, no-KYC ethos as the protocol itself.
discordPage.ctaAria: Open Vaipakam Discord invite in a new tab
discordPage.finePrintSuffix: in a new tab. The Vaipakam team never asks for wallet signatures, seed phrases, or KYC on Discord — anyone who DMs claiming to is impersonating the team.

cardHelp.dashboardYourEscrow: A dedicated UUPS-proxy escrow contract on this chain. Every user gets one. All collateral, lent assets, and locked VPFI live here — never commingled with other users.
cardHelp.dashboardYourLoans: Every loan the connected wallet is part of on this chain — as lender, borrower, or both. Each row links to the full position page with HF, LTV, collateral, repay, and claim controls.
cardHelp.dashboardVpfiPanel: VPFI is the protocol token. This card shows the wallet + escrow balance on this chain, the share of circulating supply, and the remaining mintable cap. Canonical chain mints/burns; mirror chains lock/release via LayerZero OFT.
cardHelp.dashboardFeeDiscountConsent: Opt-in for the protocol to pay discounted fees in VPFI from the escrow. Discount scales with the escrow VPFI balance: {{tier1Discount}}% at Tier 1 (≥{{tier1Min}} VPFI), {{tier2Discount}}% Tier 2 (≥{{tier2Min}}), {{tier3Discount}}% Tier 3 (≥{{tier3Min}}), {{tier4Discount}}% Tier 4 (>{{tier4Min}}).
cardHelp.dashboardRewardsSummary: Combined view of your VPFI rewards across both streams: staking yield ({{apr}}% APR on escrow VPFI) and platform-interaction rewards (daily VPFI share for every loan you're part of). Click a row to jump to the full claim card on the matching page.
cardHelp.offerBookFilters: Narrow the market lists below by asset, side, status, and other criteria. Filters apply to Lender / Borrower offers — the connected wallet's own active offers (shown above) are always visible regardless.
cardHelp.offerBookYourActiveOffers: Offers the connected wallet created on this chain that haven't been filled yet. Cancel any time before someone accepts; once accepted, the loan lifecycle moves to "Your Loans" on the Dashboard.
cardHelp.offerBookLenderOffers: Offers posted by lenders ready to lend. A borrower accepting one initiates a loan: the borrower's collateral is locked, the principal asset arrives in the borrower's wallet, and interest accrues until repayment. HF must be ≥ 1.5 at initiation.
cardHelp.offerBookBorrowerOffers: Offers posted by borrowers who've locked their collateral and are waiting for a lender. A lender accepting one funds the loan with the principal asset; the lender earns the offer's rate over the duration, less the {{treasuryFee}}% treasury cut on interest.
cardHelp.createOfferOfferType: Pick a side of the market: Lender (lender supplies the principal asset and earns interest) or Borrower (borrower locks collateral and requests principal). Rental sub-type lets NFT owners rent out ERC-721 / ERC-1155-rentable NFTs for a daily fee instead of a loan.
cardHelp.createOfferLendingAssetLender: The principal asset and amount the lender is willing to offer, plus the interest rate (APR in %) and duration in days. Rate is fixed at offer time; duration sets the grace window before the loan can default.
cardHelp.createOfferLendingAssetBorrower: The principal asset and amount the borrower wants from the lender, plus the interest rate (APR in %) and duration in days. Rate is fixed at offer time; duration sets the grace window before the loan can default.
cardHelp.createOfferNftDetails: The NFT being rented out + the daily rental fee. ERC-4907 (ERC-721 rentable) and ERC-1155-rentable standards are supported; the renter pre-pays duration × daily-fee × (1 + {{rentalBuffer}}% buffer) on accept.
cardHelp.createOfferCollateralLender: How much the lender wants the borrower to lock to secure the loan. Liquid ERC-20s (Chainlink feed + ≥$1M v3 pool depth) get LTV/HF math; illiquid ERC-20s and NFTs have no on-chain valuation and require both parties to consent to a full-collateral-on-default outcome.
cardHelp.createOfferCollateralBorrower: How much the borrower is willing to lock to secure the loan. Liquid ERC-20s (Chainlink feed + ≥$1M v3 pool depth) get LTV/HF math; illiquid ERC-20s and NFTs have no on-chain valuation and require both parties to consent to a full-collateral-on-default outcome.
cardHelp.createOfferRiskDisclosuresLender: Acknowledge the risks before signing as the lender. Smart-contract risk; oracle risk (a stale feed can delay liquidation against the principal); liquidation slippage shaves what is recovered; illiquid-collateral defaults transfer the collateral as-is with no recourse if it's worth less than the loan. Vaipakam is non-custodial — no support desk can undo a bad position.
cardHelp.createOfferRiskDisclosuresBorrower: Acknowledge the risks before signing as the borrower. Smart-contract risk; oracle risk (a stale feed can trigger liquidation at a bad moment); liquidations sell the collateral at slippage-eaten prices; illiquid-collateral defaults transfer the collateral to the lender in full with no claim left behind. Vaipakam is non-custodial — no support desk can undo a bad position.
cardHelp.createOfferAdvancedOptions: Fine-tune the offer parameters most users leave at defaults: offer expiry, fee-discount opt-in, and any role-specific options the protocol exposes. Safe to skip on a first offer.
cardHelp.claimCenterClaimsLender: Funds become claimable after a loan settles. The lender claim unlocks the principal back plus accrued interest, less the {{treasuryFee}}% treasury cut on interest. The claim consumes the lender position NFT — once claimed, the loan is done from the lender's side.
cardHelp.claimCenterClaimsBorrower: Funds become claimable after a loan settles. On full repayment the borrower claim returns the collateral; on default or liquidation, only any unused VPFI rebate from the Loan Initiation Fee. The claim consumes the borrower position NFT — once claimed, the loan is done from the borrower's side.
cardHelp.refinanceOverview: Roll an existing loan into a new one without unwinding collateral. The borrower posts a Borrower offer for the refinance terms; once a lender accepts, the protocol atomically pays off the old loan and opens the new one in a single transaction. HF must be ≥ 1.5 on the new terms for the swap to land.
cardHelp.refinancePositionSummary: Snapshot of the loan being refinanced — outstanding principal, interest accrued, current HF/LTV, and the collateral that stays locked across the swap. Use these numbers to size the new offer.
cardHelp.refinanceStep1PostOffer: Step 1 — post a Borrower offer for the new terms (asset, amount, APR, duration, collateral). Other lenders see this in the Offer Book; the old loan stays open and accruing until a lender accepts.
cardHelp.refinanceStep2Complete: Step 2 — once a lender accepts the refinance offer, complete the swap. The protocol atomically (a) repays the old loan from the new principal, (b) opens the new loan, (c) keeps the collateral in escrow throughout. No window where the position is unsecured.
cardHelp.precloseOverview: Close a loan early — before the duration ends. Two paths: Direct (repay the full outstanding balance now) or Offset (sell some collateral to cover the balance, get the remainder back). Useful when the position is no longer wanted open or to free collateral early.
cardHelp.preclosePositionSummary: Snapshot of the loan being closed early — outstanding principal, interest accrued so far, current HF/LTV. Pre-Phase-5 a flat early-close penalty applied; in Phase 5 the time-weighted VPFI rebate / fee math handles it without a separate penalty.
cardHelp.precloseInProgress: An offset preclose is mid-flight — collateral is being sold via the swap router. Complete it (settles the loan from the proceeds) or, if the price moved, cancel and try again at a fresh quote.
cardHelp.precloseChoosePath: Direct: pay the full outstanding balance now from the wallet. Offset: sell part of the collateral on a DEX, use the proceeds to repay, get whatever is left back. Direct is cheaper if the cash is on hand; Offset is the path otherwise.
cardHelp.earlyWithdrawalOverview: Lender-side early-exit. Sell the loan's claim NFT through the protocol before the duration ends. The position NFT (which represents the right to repayment + interest) is auctioned to a buyer; the seller receives the sale proceeds, the buyer takes over the lender side.
cardHelp.earlyWithdrawalPositionSummary: Snapshot of the lender position being exited — principal, accrued interest, time remaining, current HF/LTV of the borrower. These numbers anchor the price the buyer is willing to pay.
cardHelp.earlyWithdrawalInitiateSale: Step 1 — initiate the sale. The protocol lists the lender NFT for the asking price set; once a buyer accepts, the proceeds settle to the seller's wallet and the buyer becomes the new lender of record. Cancellation is allowed before a buyer accepts.
cardHelp.publicDashboardOverview: Aggregated protocol metrics derived entirely from on-chain contract state and event logs across every supported chain. No wallet required, no off-chain database — figures recompute live from the Diamond.
cardHelp.publicDashboardCombined: Top-line totals summed across every supported chain. The chains-covered / chains-unreachable counts indicate whether any RPC failed at fetch time — the per-chain table below shows which.
cardHelp.publicDashboardPerChain: Per-chain split of the same metrics. Useful for spotting which chain is carrying TVL or loan volume, and for confirming that mirror-chain VPFI supplies sum to the canonical lock balance.
cardHelp.publicDashboardVpfiTransparency: On-chain VPFI accounting on this chain: total supply, circulating supply (after subtracting protocol-held balances), and the live mintable cap. Canonical chain mints/burns; mirror chains lock/release via the OFT adapter so total cross-chain supply stays bounded.
cardHelp.publicDashboardTransparency: Provenance of every figure on this page. Snapshot block, data freshness, the Diamond address each metric was read from, and the exact view function. Any number on this page can be re-derived from the linked block + function call.
cardHelp.keeperSettingsOverview: Keepers are delegated managers of the user's role on a loan. Approving a keeper here delegates only one side, only for the actions authorised. Liquidations, repayments, adding collateral, and claiming stay user-only — keepers cannot touch money-out paths. Up to 5 approved keepers per wallet.
cardHelp.keeperSettingsApprovedList: Each approved keeper carries a bitmask of permitted actions: complete loan sale, complete offset, init early-withdraw, init preclose, refinance. Edit a keeper to flip individual action bits. Removing a keeper revokes the master entry on-chain — they instantly stop being able to act on every loan.
cardHelp.nftVerifierLookup: Before buying a Vaipakam position NFT from another holder, paste the contract address + token ID here. The verifier confirms whether it was minted by Vaipakam, which chain it lives on, the position's current state, and the on-chain owner. The NFT is the bearer instrument for the loan's claim — losing it loses the claim.
cardHelp.alertsOverview: Get a heads-up when the Health Factor falls toward the liquidation threshold. An off-chain watcher polls active loans every 5 min and fires on band crossings — no gas, no on-chain state. Alerts fire once per downgrade; climbing back to healthy re-arms the ladder.
cardHelp.alertsThresholdLadder: The HF bands that trigger alerts. Crossing into a more-dangerous band (e.g. 1.5 → 1.2) fires once. The next alert only fires after another band is crossed. Climbing back above a band rearms it. Defaults are tuned for liquid loans; tune higher when carrying volatile collateral.
cardHelp.alertsDeliveryChannels: Where alerts get delivered — Telegram bot DM and / or Push Protocol wallet notifications. Both rails share the same threshold ladder above; per-channel warn-levels are intentionally not exposed. Enable one or both.
cardHelp.allowancesList: Every ERC-20 approval the wallet has granted the Vaipakam Diamond on this chain. Revoke any approval no longer needed in one click — sets allowance to zero on-chain. Non-zero approvals appear first; the zero rows below are a reference so the list can be confirmed clean.
cardHelp.loanDetailsOverview: Single-loan view. Shows terms, live HF/LTV, collateral, parties, and every action available to the viewer on this loan — repay, claim, liquidate, preclose, refinance — depending on status and role (lender / borrower / third-party liquidator).
cardHelp.loanDetailsTerms: Principal asset, original amount, fixed APR (bps), duration, and the derived interest accrued so far. Terms are immutable once the loan is initiated; refinancing creates a new loan rather than mutating this one.
cardHelp.loanDetailsCollateralRiskLender: The collateral securing the loan + the live Health Factor and LTV. Liquid collateral has oracle-backed valuation and is auto-liquidated when HF drops below 1; illiquid collateral has no on-chain valuation and is transferred to the lender in full on default. Watch HF — when it drops, the lender's protection thins.
cardHelp.loanDetailsCollateralRiskBorrower: The collateral the borrower has locked + the live Health Factor and LTV. Keep HF above 1 — if it drops below, the collateral can be liquidated. Liquid collateral is auto-priced via oracle; illiquid collateral has no on-chain valuation and is transferred to the lender in full on default.
cardHelp.loanDetailsParties: Lender and borrower addresses, the per-user escrow proxies that hold their assets, and the position NFTs each side received at loan-init. Each NFT is the bearer instrument for that side's claim — losing it loses the claim.
cardHelp.loanDetailsActionsLender: Actions available to the lender on this loan. Claim once the loan settles (returns principal + interest, less the {{treasuryFee}}% treasury cut). Initiate Early Withdrawal to sell the lender NFT to a buyer mid-loan. Anyone (including the lender) can trigger Liquidate when HF < 1 or the grace period expires. Disabled actions show a hover reason.
cardHelp.loanDetailsActionsBorrower: Actions available to the borrower on this loan. Repay full or partial; Preclose direct (pay outstanding now) or offset (sell collateral via DEX); Refinance into a new loan with new terms. Claim returns the collateral on full repayment, or any unused VPFI rebate. Disabled actions show a hover reason.
cardHelp.buyVpfiOverview: Purchase VPFI at the fixed early-stage rate using ETH on any supported chain. The canonical chain (Base) routes direct to the Diamond; every other chain bridges via VPFIBuyAdapter + LayerZero round-trip. VPFI always lands in the wallet on the chain connected from — no chain switch required.
cardHelp.buyVpfiDiscountStatus: The active VPFI fee-discount tier on this chain. Tier is set by the escrow VPFI balance: Tier 1 ≥{{tier1Min}}, Tier 2 ≥{{tier2Min}}, Tier 3 ≥{{tier3Min}}, Tier 4 >{{tier4Min}}. Discount only applies on liquid loans and only while the platform-level consent toggle on the Dashboard is on. Escrow VPFI also counts as staked and earns the {{apr}}% staking APR.
cardHelp.buyVpfiBuy: Step 1 — convert ETH to VPFI. On the canonical chain the call goes directly to the Diamond; off-canonical it goes through the buy adapter and a LayerZero round-trip. Per-tx and rolling caps apply (see TokenomicsTechSpec). VPFI is delivered to the wallet, not auto-deposited into escrow.
cardHelp.buyVpfiDeposit: Step 2 — move VPFI from the wallet into the escrow on this chain. Always an explicit user action: the protocol never auto-funds escrow after a buy. Required to qualify for fee discounts and the {{apr}}% staking APR. Permit2 is used when available so a single signature replaces the approve + transfer pair.
cardHelp.buyVpfiUnstake: Move VPFI from the escrow back to the wallet on this chain. Reduces the discount tier in real time — the time-weighted accumulator re-stamps every open loan at the new (lower) balance immediately, so an unstake is effective for fee-discount math from this moment forward.
cardHelp.rewardsOverview: Two reward streams accrue on this chain. Escrow-held VPFI earns a passive {{apr}}% APR (staking pool). Every USD of interest settled on a loan earns a daily share of the interaction pool. Both are minted directly on the current chain when claimed — no bridging.
cardHelp.rewardsClaim: Mints any pending VPFI from both reward streams (staking + interaction) into the wallet, on the current chain, in one transaction. The interaction pool finalizes daily — pending interaction rewards become claimable shortly after each settlement window closes.
cardHelp.rewardsWithdrawStaked: Move VPFI out of the escrow back to the wallet. Withdrawn VPFI stops accruing the {{apr}}% staking APR and no longer counts toward the fee-discount tier — the time-weighted accumulator immediately re-stamps every open loan at the new (lower) balance.
cardHelp.activityFeed: On-chain events involving the connected wallet on the active chain — offers created or accepted, loans, repayments, claims, liquidations, NFT mints/burns, VPFI buys/stakes/unstakes. Sourced live from Diamond event logs (no backend), grouped by transaction and ordered newest-first.

vpfiDiscountConsent.title: Fee-discount consent
vpfiDiscountConsent.bodyPrefix: When enabled, the protocol may deduct VPFI from the escrow to pay the discounted fee on both the borrower Loan Initiation Fee and / or the lender Yield Fee. The discount scales with the escrow VPFI balance — {{tier1Discount}}% off at Tier 1 (≥ {{tier1Min}} VPFI), {{tier2Discount}}% at Tier 2 (≥ {{tier2Min}}), {{tier3Discount}}% at Tier 3 (≥ {{tier3Min}}), and {{tier4Discount}}% at Tier 4 (> {{tier4Min}}). Acquire and deposit VPFI on

lenderDiscountCard.borrowerTitle: Borrowing a liquid ERC-20? Earn up to a {{maxDiscount}}% VPFI rebate on the initiation fee
lenderDiscountCard.borrowerBody1: Hold VPFI in the personal escrow and enable the one-time platform-level VPFI consent on the Dashboard. The protocol pulls the full {{loanInitiationFee}}% initiation fee up front in VPFI, then credits back a time-weighted rebate at proper loan close (repay / preclose / refinance) based on how long the borrower held VPFI across the loan. Default or liquidation forfeits the rebate. Need VPFI?
lenderDiscountCard.lenderTitle: Lending a liquid ERC-20? Earn up to a {{maxDiscount}}% VPFI discount on the yield fee
lenderDiscountCard.lenderBody1: Hold VPFI in the personal escrow and enable the one-time platform-level VPFI consent on the Dashboard. At settlement, the {{treasuryFee}}% treasury cut on interest earned is reduced by a time-weighted VPFI discount across the loan's lifetime — up to {{maxDiscount}}%, so the lender keeps more of the interest earned. Nothing is paid up front; the discount is applied automatically when the loan settles properly (repay / preclose / refinance). Default or liquidation forfeits the discount. Need VPFI?
lenderDiscountCard.routingNote: (buy from a preferred chain — routing is handled).
lenderDiscountCard.tiersDifferAlertPrefix: At settlement the treasury cut on this loan's yield is reduced by the time-weighted average
lenderDiscountCard.tiersDifferAlertSuffix: rate. Topping up VPFI just before repay won't capture the full current-tier discount — only the share proportional to how long it was held during the loan.
lenderDiscountCard.consentMissingBody: You haven't given consent to use staked / deposited VPFI for fee discounts on this loan. The discount tier is opt-in — until you enable it on the Dashboard, the yield-fee on settlement will be charged at the full {{treasuryFee}}% with no VPFI rebate.
lenderDiscountCard.consentEnabledNoVpfiBody: You've given consent, but no eligible VPFI is currently staked / deposited in your escrow on this chain. Top up VPFI to start earning a time-weighted discount on the yield fee.

vpfiTokenCard.title: VPFI Token (this chain)
vpfiTokenCard.canonicalTip: Canonical chain — the VPFIToken contract and the OFT Adapter live here. Cross-chain bridges lock the canonical supply on the way out and release it on the way back; total supply is fixed at this chain.
vpfiTokenCard.mirrorTip: Mirror chain — VPFI on this chain is a 1:1 representation of the canonical supply. The OFT mints tokens here when a bridge transfer arrives and burns them when a transfer leaves, so the mirror balance is always backed by canonical-chain locks.
vpfiTokenCard.notRegistered: VPFI is not yet registered with the Diamond on {{chain}}. Once the admin calls setVPFIToken, balances and activity will appear here.
vpfiTokenCard.escrowTooltip: VPFI currently staked in the per-user escrow on this chain. Counts toward the borrower fee discount tier.
vpfiTokenCard.shareTooltip: Effective ownership of this chain's VPFI supply — wallet balance plus escrow-locked balance, divided by circulating. Escrow VPFI is still owned by the user; it's just locked for tier discount / {{apr}}% APR.
vpfiTokenCard.noActivity: No VPFI transfers touch this wallet on {{chain}} yet. User-facing distributions (rewards, staking) arrive in a later rollout phase.
vpfiTokenCard.escrowCountsAsStaked: Escrow VPFI counts as staked ({{apr}}% APR)

buyVpfiCards.escrowCountsAsStaked: Escrow VPFI counts as staked ({{apr}}% APR)
buyVpfiCards.inactiveBelowTier1: Inactive · below Tier 1 ({{tier1Min}} VPFI in escrow)
buyVpfiCards.depositGapAchieved: The escrow balance qualifies for {{tier}} on the next loan. Ensure the discount consent is enabled above.
buyVpfiCards.enableSharedConsent: Enable the shared discount consent on the <dashboardLink>Dashboard</dashboardLink>.
buyVpfiCards.depositMore: Deposit {{amount}} more VPFI into escrow to reach Tier {{tier}} ({{discount}}).
buyVpfiCards.buySubtitle: {{chain}} directly. VPFI lands in the wallet on this chain.
buyVpfiCards.gasReserveTooltip: A small gas reserve ({{reserve}} ETH) is held back from the wallet balance so the buy tx can still be broadcast.
buyVpfiCards.capExceeded: This amount exceeds the remaining cap. Reduce the ETH input and try again.
buyVpfiCards.exceedsBalance: Amount exceeds the wallet ETH balance of {{balance}} ETH (a small gas reserve is kept aside).
buyVpfiCards.exceedsEscrowBalance: Amount exceeds the escrow VPFI balance of {{balance}} VPFI.
buyVpfiCards.depositSubtitle: Required on every chain — including the canonical one.
buyVpfiCards.depositSpecNote: By spec, transferring VPFI into escrow is always an explicit user action. The protocol never auto-funds the escrow after a buy or bridge.
buyVpfiCards.unstakeSubtitle: Move VPFI from the escrow on {{chain}} back to the wallet. If the escrow balance falls below a tier threshold, the discount tier drops too.
buyVpfiCards.unstakeWarning: Escrow VPFI counts toward the borrower fee discount tier and earns {{apr}}% APR. Unstaking moves tokens back to the wallet — if the remaining escrow balance drops below a tier threshold the discount drops with it on the next loan.

alertsPage.subtitle: Get a heads-up when the Health Factor (HF) drops toward the liquidation threshold. The off-chain watcher polls active loans every 5 minutes and alerts on band crossings — no gas, no on-chain state. Alerts fire once per downgrade; recovering to a healthier band re-arms the ladder.
alertsPage.thresholdLadderBody: A fresh alert fires the first time HF crosses each band downward. Once a band fires it re-arms after HF recovers above the next higher band's threshold. Default values are conservative — adjust to taste.
alertsPage.warnDescription: First heads-up — plenty of runway still.
alertsPage.alertDescription: Meaningful — take a look at adding collateral or repaying.
alertsPage.criticalDescription: Liquidation imminent — HF < 1.00 triggers it on-chain.
alertsPage.deliveryChannelBody: Enable one or both. Both rails share the threshold ladder above — different warn-levels per channel are not supported.
alertsPage.telegramBody: Universal — no wallet plug-in required. Handshake: request a code here, DM it to the bot, confirmation arrives back in Telegram.
alertsPage.pushBody: On-chain notification channel delivered to Push-enabled wallets (Rabby, Push Wallet, MetaMask with Push Snap). Two steps: (1) enable the rail here so the watcher dispatches Push notifications for the wallet, and (2) subscribe to the Vaipakam channel from a Push-enabled wallet so the notifications actually land.
alertsPage.telegramHandshakeBody: DM this code to the Vaipakam alerts (@VaipakamBot) bot within 10 minutes:

allowancesPage.subtitle: Every ERC-20 approval granted to the Vaipakam Diamond by the connected wallet on this chain. Revoke ones no longer needed in one click. Non-zero allowances appear first; zero-allowance rows below are a reference so the list can be confirmed clean.
allowancesPage.footnote: This page only scans the Vaipakam Diamond as the spender on the current chain. Approvals to other contracts (DEX aggregators, other dApps) are not listed here — use revoke.cash or a similar tool to audit them.

consent.dismissAria: Dismiss — we'll ask again next visit
consent.title: Your privacy choices
consent.body: We use cookies and similar technologies to run essential parts of the app (session state, anti-abuse) and — with permission — to understand how Vaipakam is used so it can be improved. The choice can be changed at any time from the "Cookie settings" link in the footer.
consent.categoryEssentialDesc: Required for the app to function — session state, wallet connection, anti-abuse. Cannot be disabled.
consent.categoryAnalyticsDesc: Anonymous usage stats that help understand which features matter and where flows break.
consent.categoryPersonalizationDesc: Remembers UI preferences across visits (beyond the essentials).
consent.categoryAdvertisingDesc: Ad measurement and targeting. Vaipakam does not currently serve ads, but this covers any future ad-measurement pixels.

interestWarning.earlyWithdrawalBody: By selling your lender position now, you give up all interest accrued so far on this loan. The forfeited amount goes to the protocol treasury, or — if the new lender offers a lower rate — is applied toward the rate shortfall first. You walk away with the principal you funded (minus any shortfall to the new lender), but not the interest earned to date.
interestWarning.precloseDirectBody: Closing the loan now via the Direct path requires you to pay the FULL TERM interest as if the loan ran to maturity — not just the interest accrued so far. There is no early-close discount on this path. The lender receives 99% of that interest; 1% goes to the protocol treasury.
interestWarning.precloseTransferTitle: You'll pay accrued interest plus a rate shortfall
interestWarning.precloseTransferBody: Transferring the obligation to a new borrower requires you to pay the interest accrued to date plus a shortfall — the difference between what the lender expected to earn over the remaining term at your original rate and what they will earn from the new borrower's offer. If the new offer matches your rate, the shortfall is zero.
interestWarning.precloseOffsetTitle: You'll pay accrued interest plus shortfall, and lock new principal
interestWarning.precloseOffsetBody: The Offset path settles your loan against a fresh lender offer that you create. Up front you pay the interest accrued so far plus any rate shortfall to the original lender, and you must deposit fresh principal as collateral for the offset offer. Your borrower-side NFT is locked until a new borrower accepts the offer or you cancel it.
interestWarning.refinanceTitle: Old loan closes at full-term interest plus shortfall
interestWarning.refinanceBody: Refinancing repays the old lender with principal plus FULL TERM interest (not just accrued) plus any rate shortfall — payable when the new offer's expected interest is lower than the old loan's. You're effectively paying as if the old loan reached maturity, in exchange for the new terms.

transferLockWarning.preconfirmBodyLender: Submitting this transaction will lock the lender-side Vaipakam NFT #{{tokenId}} from transfer and approval until this {{flow}} flow completes or is cancelled. While locked, the NFT cannot be moved to another wallet, listed, sold, or approved on any marketplace, and its ownership-driven actions remain bound to this in-progress flow.
transferLockWarning.preconfirmBodyBorrower: Submitting this transaction will lock the borrower-side Vaipakam NFT #{{tokenId}} from transfer and approval until this {{flow}} flow completes or is cancelled. While locked, the NFT cannot be moved to another wallet, listed, sold, or approved on any marketplace, and its ownership-driven actions remain bound to this in-progress flow.
transferLockWarning.preconfirmCrossRoleNote: Note: this caller is acting as the current NFT holder — the lock applies to whoever owns the NFT at submission time.
transferLockWarning.activeBody: This position NFT is currently locked by an in-progress {{reason}} flow. It cannot be transferred or approved until the responsible flow completes or the linked offer is cancelled.

riskDisclosures.title: Risk Disclosures
riskDisclosures.checkboxLabel: I have read and agree to the Risk Disclosures above.
riskDisclosures.section1Heading: If liquidation of liquid collateral fails (like in Abnormal Market conditions, when slippage > 6%, thin liquidity, DEX revert, or any other runtime failure)
riskDisclosures.section1Point1: Lender receives the collateral in-kind — NOT the lending asset.
riskDisclosures.section1Point2: If collateral value < amount due: the lender receives ALL collateral; the borrower receives nothing; no shortfall top-up.
riskDisclosures.section1Point3: If collateral value ≥ amount due: the lender receives collateral equal to the amount owed at oracle price; remainder returns to the borrower after charges.
riskDisclosures.section2Heading: If the borrower defaults on a loan with illiquid assets (illiquid lending asset, illiquid collateral, or both)
riskDisclosures.section2Point1: Full collateral transfers to the lender in-kind, regardless of its market value.
riskDisclosures.section2Point2: No partition between "amount owed" and "remainder" — the lender takes ALL collateral.
riskDisclosures.section2Point3: The asset received may be worth materially more or less than the amount owed. No warranty on value, liquidity, or resaleability.
riskDisclosures.section3Point1: Recovery may be materially less than the asset lent. The lender may receive assets other than the one lent. No further claim on the borrower, the protocol, its contributors, or any third party.
riskDisclosures.section3Point2: Proceeding records a binding agreement on-chain against the wallet for the full life of this position.

settings.title: Settings
settings.modeInfo: Basic hides advanced pages and controls like keeper settings. Switch to Advanced once you're comfortable with the core lending flow.

dataRights.title: Your data rights (GDPR / UK GDPR / CCPA)
dataRights.subtitle: Export or erase the data Vaipakam stores in your browser. On-chain positions are never affected — Vaipakam has no power to erase blockchain state.
dataRights.downloadBody: Exports every Vaipakam-namespaced key from your browser as a portable JSON file. Covers:
dataRights.downloadBullet1: Journey-log telemetry (sessionStorage — last few minutes of UI events for support diagnostics).
dataRights.downloadBullet3: Cached event index (per-chain offer / loan IDs the app re-uses to avoid full chain re-scans).
dataRights.deleteBody: Removes every Vaipakam-namespaced key from your browser. Click once to arm the action; click again on the now-red button to confirm.
dataRights.deleteEffect1: The cookie / consent banner returns on the next page load.
dataRights.deleteEffect2: Your local activity-log buffer is wiped (no support diagnostics until new events accumulate).
dataRights.deleteEffect3: Cached event indexes are cleared — the next dashboard / offer-book load re-scans the chain from scratch (slower than usual).
dataRights.deleteEffect4: Theme, language, mode (basic/advanced) and any other Vaipakam-namespaced preferences reset to defaults.
dataRights.deleteOnChainNote: On-chain positions, loans, and balances are unaffected. Vaipakam cannot erase blockchain state.
dataRights.deleteConfirmCta: Click again to confirm — this is irreversible
dataRights.journeyBody: Saves the in-memory journey-log buffer (UI events from this tab's session) as a JSON file. Useful for sharing in a Discord DM or support thread. Independent of the broader Download / Delete above — those touch your stored namespace; this captures only the live event buffer.
dataRights.legalFooter: Vaipakam acts as data controller for the items above. We do not maintain any server-side personal data (no accounts, no email lists, no analytics linked to your wallet). For audit purposes we record every UI error event server-side with a per-event UUID — see the Privacy Policy for what's captured.

createOffer.subtitleBorrower: Create a borrowing offer — specify what you need and the collateral you can provide.

validate.amountNonPositive: Amount must be greater than zero.
validate.durationOutOfRange: Duration must be between {{min}} and {{max}} days.

createOffer.hintCollateralAssetTypeAuto: Auto-detected from the collateral contract address.
createOffer.hintNftAddressManual: Enter the NFT collection's contract address manually.
createOffer.hintAddressLocked: Pinned to the original loan — the settlement reverts on a mismatch.
createOffer.hintAmountTokens: Amount in whole tokens (scaled on-chain using the token's decimals)
createOffer.hintAmountMax: Optional ceiling — bots can match any amount in [min, max]. Leave blank for a single-amount offer.
createOffer.hintInterestRateMax: Optional ceiling — bots match at the midpoint of the overlapping rate range. Leave blank for a single rate.
createOffer.prepayAssetHint: Stablecoin used for rental fee prepayment + {{rentalBuffer}}% buffer
createOffer.lockAssetsAlert: Creating an offer will lock the assets in the personal escrow and mint a Vaipakam position NFT. The accepting party pays gas to initiate the loan.
createOffer.lifLenderBody: When the offer is accepted, {{loanInitiationFee}}% of the lending amount will be routed to the Vaipakam treasury before the borrower receives the remaining {{borrowerNet}}%. The borrower still repays the full principal.
createOffer.lifBorrowerBody: When the request is funded, {{loanInitiationFee}}% of the lending amount will be routed to the Vaipakam treasury before the borrower receives the remaining {{borrowerNet}}%. The borrower still repays the full principal.
createOffer.keeperAccessLabel: Enable authorized keeper / third-party execution access
createOffer.keeperAccessHintMid1: flag only. By itself it is
createOffer.keeperAccessHintMid2: sufficient — before any keeper can act on the offer creator's behalf the creator must
createOffer.keeperAccessHintSuffix: enable keeper access and add the keeper to the whitelist in the advanced profile (Keepers page). Keeper authority is role-scoped: the offer creator's own profile opt-in + whitelist govern only the actions the creator's side is entitled to.
createOffer.allowsPartialRepayHintLender: When enabled, the borrower can call repayPartial on the resulting loan to pay down principal in chunks before the term ends. Default off — when off, only full repayment is accepted on-chain.
createOffer.allowsPartialRepayHintBorrower: Request the right to repay this loan in partial chunks. The lender consents implicitly by accepting your offer; if they don't want partial repay they will skip it. Default off.

loanDetails.fallbackPendingBorrower: Borrower may still cure it by repaying in full, or by adding collateral until Health Factor and LTV return to their initiation thresholds.
loanDetails.fallbackPendingLender: Borrower may still cure the position (full repay or collateral top-up) until the lender finalizes the claim.
loanDetails.repayDescBorrower: Repay the principal + interest in full to close the loan and release the collateral.
loanDetails.repayDescOther: Anyone can repay this loan on behalf of the borrower (full repayment only).

loanDetails.repayConfirmReminderBody: repaying this loan does not grant collateral rights. Collateral is claimable only by the current holder of borrower NFT #{{tokenId}}.
loanDetails.addCollateralFallbackDesc: Top up collateral to cure the fallback. The loan reactivates once Health Factor and LTV are back within their initiation thresholds.
loanDetails.addCollateralNormalDesc: Add more collateral to improve the Health Factor and avoid liquidation.
loanDetails.liquidateHfDesc: This loan is undercollateralized. Any wallet may liquidate — collateral is swapped to principal across the best-priced DEX and the lender is made whole. The liquidator collects an incentive (up to 3% of proceeds, tapering with realized slippage).
loanDetails.triggerDefaultDesc: This loan is overdue. Anyone can trigger the default process.
loanDetails.earlyWithdrawalDesc: Exit this loan before maturity by selling the lender position to a new lender. Initiating this flow will lock the lender NFT from transfer until the sale completes or is cancelled.
loanDetails.precloseDesc: Close this loan early by repaying directly, or by offsetting with a new lender offer. The offset path will lock the borrower NFT from transfer until the new offer is accepted or cancelled.
loanDetails.refinanceDesc: Switch to a new lender with better terms by posting a borrower offer and completing the refinance once accepted.
loanDetails.noKeepersWhitelistPrefix: No keepers on the global whitelist.

buyVpfi.title: Buy VPFI

preconnect.tagline: Pay lower fees on your loans, earn yield on what you stake.
preconnect.whatBody1: VPFI is Vaipakam's protocol token — you can buy with ETH, hold in your personal escrow, and use across the platform. No lockup period, freely transferable.
preconnect.whatBody2: Holding VPFI ties you to the protocol's success in two practical ways: it earns you discounts on the fees Vaipakam charges (so you keep more of what you lend, and pay less when you borrow), and it earns staking yield while it sits in your escrow.
preconnect.whatBody3: Anyone with ETH can buy and stake. The two benefit cards below show exactly what holding VPFI gets you.
preconnect.discountBody: VPFI you hold in your personal escrow earns you a time-weighted discount on Vaipakam's protocol fees — applied automatically when each loan settles, no separate claim step:
preconnect.discountBullet1: Lenders: a discount on the treasury cut taken from interest earned. The more VPFI you've held across the loan's lifetime, the smaller the cut.
preconnect.discountBullet2: Borrowers: a VPFI rebate on the loan-initiation fee, paid back at proper close (repay / preclose / refinance). Default forfeits the rebate.
preconnect.stakingBody: Any VPFI sitting in your personal escrow earns staking APR for as long as it stays there. No loan required — staking is open to everyone, and the same balance also counts toward your fee-discount tier.
preconnect.howBody: Buy VPFI with ETH at the fixed early-stage rate, then deposit it into your escrow to start earning. Cross-chain routing is handled for you — VPFI lands on whichever chain you bought from.
preconnect.analyticsHint: Looking for protocol-wide stats and circulating supply? See the
preconnect.launchAppCta: Launch App to Buy / Stake / Unstake
preconnect.launchAppHint: Opens the app in a new tab. The buy, stake, and unstake controls all live there once your wallet is connected.

buyVpfi.connectBody: Connect your wallet to buy, stake, or unstake VPFI. The same page handles all three — your wallet decides which action you can take.
buyVpfi.unsupportedNetworkBody: Buy VPFI is available on any supported Vaipakam chain. Your wallet is currently on a network that does not host a Vaipakam Diamond. Switch to a supported chain to continue.
buyVpfi.pageSubtitle: Purchase VPFI at the fixed early-stage rate using ETH and deposit / stake into your escrow to earn staking rewards — and unlock the tiered discount on liquid loans.
buyVpfi.step1SubtitleCanonical: Direct on {{chain}}. VPFI lands in your wallet on this chain.
buyVpfi.step1SubtitleBridged: Direct from {{chain}} — any cross-chain routing is handled for you. VPFI lands in your wallet on this chain.
buyVpfi.step1SubtitlePending: The buy adapter is being deployed to {{chain}}. You can continue here — no chain switch required.
buyVpfi.step2Title: Deposit / Stake VPFI into your escrow
buyVpfi.step2Info: Staking is open to everyone — you don't need a loan to participate. Any VPFI you deposit into your escrow earns {{apr}}% APR for as long as it stays there. First time staking? Your escrow is created for you automatically on your first deposit — no setup needed.
buyVpfi.step3Title: Withdraw / Unstake VPFI from your escrow
buyVpfi.escrowBalanceZero: Your escrow VPFI balance is 0 — deposit VPFI first.

alerts.connectBody: Connect your wallet to configure HF alerts.
alerts.switchChainBody: Switch to a supported chain to configure alerts for your loans.
alerts.pageSubtitle: Get a heads-up when your Health Factor (HF) falls toward the liquidation threshold. The off-chain watcher polls your active loans every 5 minutes and alerts on band crossings — no gas, no on-chain state. Alerts fire once per downgrade; climbing back to healthy re-arms the ladder.

nftVerifier.pageSubtitle: Verify the authenticity of a Vaipakam position NFT before you buy it from another holder. Paste the contract address and token ID from the marketplace listing — we'll confirm whether it was minted by Vaipakam, which chain it lives on and the position's current state with NFT's current Owner, so you can decide whether to purchase.
nftVerifier.contractAddressHint: Paste the NFT collection address from the marketplace. The Verifier checks it against every Vaipakam deployment to confirm the NFT is genuine and tell you which chain it sits on.
nftVerifier.loanContextUnavailable: Historical loan context isn't available in the local event index (the mint may predate the scan range).
nftVerifier.burnedNoLoanInitiated: This NFT was minted when the creator posted the offer and was burned when the offer was {{status}}. No loan was ever initiated through this specific tokenId, so there is no loan-side history to show.
nftVerifier.doNotPurchaseBurned: Do not purchase. A burned NFT has no claim rights and cannot be transferred.
nftVerifier.burnedHeader: — the position NFT is destroyed and the token ID can never be reused or transferred.

refinance.pageSubtitle: Replace the current lender with a new one offering better terms. Works as a two-step flow: post a Borrower Offer, then complete the refinance after a new lender accepts it.
refinance.phase1Erc20Only: Refinance is only supported for ERC-20 loans in Phase 1.

preclose.pageSubtitle: Close this loan before maturity: pay directly, transfer the obligation to an existing Borrower Offer, or offset with a new lender offer (borrower NFT locked until complete).
preclose.offsetTooltipPhase1: Offset path is ERC-20 only in Phase 1
preclose.directPrecloseDesc: Pay principal plus full-term interest now. The loan closes immediately and the borrower's collateral becomes claimable. No Vaipakam NFT lock — this path is atomic.
preclose.transferReviewBody: Confirm moves the loan obligation to the new borrower atomically: the borrower repays accrued interest + any rate shortfall + treasury fee, the new borrower's collateral (already locked in their escrow) backs the live loan, and the original collateral becomes claimable — all in one transaction. No NFT transfer-lock is required.
preclose.transferIntroBody: Pick an existing Borrower Offer from the Offer Book and hand off this loan to its creator. Contract-enforced rules (PrecloseFacet.transferObligationViaOffer):
preclose.transferRuleSameAssets: Same lending, collateral, collateral-type, and prepay asset as this loan
preclose.transferRuleAmountEqual: Offer amount must equal this loan's principal exactly
preclose.transferRuleDurationLte: Offer duration ≤ remaining days on this loan
preclose.transferRuleNewBorrower: New borrower must be a different address and pass sanctions / KYC
preclose.offsetIntroBody: Post a new lender offer linked to this loan. As soon as a replacement borrower accepts it, the offset completes atomically in the same transaction — the original collateral is released and the original loan closes with no extra click. The borrower-side Vaipakam NFT will be locked for transfer from submission until completion or cancellation.
preclose.requiredCollateralLabel: Required collateral (whole tokens, same asset as original)
preclose.amountWholeTokensHint: Amount in whole tokens (scaled on-chain using the token's decimals).

earlyWithdrawal.pageSubtitle: Sell the lender position to a new lender. The lender-side Vaipakam NFT will be locked for transfer while the sale is pending.
earlyWithdrawal.phase1Erc20Only: Lender-side sale is only supported for ERC-20 loans in Phase 1. NFT-rental lender positions cannot be sold through this flow.
earlyWithdrawal.saleIntroBody: Set a new interest rate that a replacement lender will see on the sale offer. A new lender can then accept the offer from the Offer Book. The lender forfeits accrued interest to treasury on completion.

publicDashboard.pageSubtitle: Transparent, aggregated protocol metrics derived entirely from on-chain contract state and event logs. No wallet required.
publicDashboard.perChainSubtitle: Metrics below are scoped to the selected chain — no wallet required. If a wallet is connected, it is also asked to switch networks so any subsequent writes land on the same chain.
publicDashboard.aggregatingCombined: Aggregating metrics from every deployed chain…

keeperSettings.title: Keeper Whitelist
keeperSettings.advancedOnlyBody: Keeper management is an advanced feature. Switch to Advanced using the mode toggle in the top bar to delegate role-scoped execution of your loan actions to trusted keeper addresses.
keeperSettings.connectBody: Connect your wallet to manage keepers.
keeperSettings.switchChainBody: Switch to a supported chain to manage keepers.
keeperSettings.pageSubtitle: Keepers are delegated managers of your role. Approving a keeper delegates only your side of a loan, and only for the specific actions you authorise. A lender's keeper with just "Complete early withdrawal" can finish an early-withdraw sale you initiated, but cannot start one on your behalf. Liquidations, repayments, adding collateral, and claiming stay user-only — keepers cannot touch money-out paths. You may whitelist up to {{max}} keeper addresses.
keeperSettings.perLoanGatesAlert: Two additional gates apply per-loan. Even after you approve a keeper here, they can only act on a specific loan when (a) your master keeper-access switch below is ON and (b) you've explicitly enabled them for that loan on the Offer Book or Loan Details page.
keeperSettings.facetUnavailable: Keeper whitelist is not enabled on the currently deployed Diamond (the keeper facet hasn't been cut in yet). Management is disabled until the deployment includes it.
keeperSettings.masterAccessBody: One-switch emergency brake — disables every whitelisted keeper on every loan without touching the allowlist.

The annotation appears only when an anchor is available for the current filters.

hero.subtitleBody: lets you lend and borrow a wide range of tokens, rent NFTs, and set your own terms. Every position is tracked by a unique NFT — transparent, traceable and trustless from offer to settlement.

features.title: Everything you need for trustless lending
features.subtitle: A complete DeFi lending protocol built on the Diamond Standard, supporting ERC-20 tokens and rentable NFTs across Ethereum, Polygon, and Arbitrum.
features.p2pDesc: Create or accept offers for ERC-20 tokens with custom terms — interest rate, collateral type, and loan duration. No intermediary pools.
features.nftDesc: Rent out ERC-721 and ERC-1155 NFTs with daily pricing. Borrowers get user rights while the NFT stays safely in escrow.
features.escrowDesc: Every user gets their own UUPS upgradeable escrow proxy. No fund commingling — assets are always isolated.
features.nftPositionDesc: Every offer and loan mints a unique Vaipakam NFT with on-chain metadata. Prove ownership, claim funds, and track status on any marketplace.
features.liquidityDesc: Chainlink oracles + on-chain DEX liquidity checks determine asset status. Liquid assets get LTV monitoring; illiquid assets use full collateral transfer.
features.liquidationDesc: Health Factor drops below threshold? Permissionless DEX liquidation kicks in. Grace period expired? Time-based default with collateral transfer.
features.precloseDesc: Borrowers can early-repay, transfer debt to another borrower, or offset with a new lender position — all while protecting the original lender.
features.alertsDesc: Platform-funded SMS and email alerts for LTV warnings, repayment reminders, liquidation events, and claimable funds.

howItWorksSection.title: From offer to settlement in four steps
howItWorksSection.subtitle: Every action is recorded on-chain. Position NFTs provide proof of ownership and are required to claim funds.
howItWorksSection.step1Desc: As a lender, specify the asset, interest rate, collateral requirements, and duration. As a borrower, state what is needed and the collateral that can be provided. Assets are locked in the personal escrow.
howItWorksSection.step1Detail3: Vaipakam NFT minted for the position
howItWorksSection.step2Desc: Browse the offer book, filter by asset type or rate, and accept a compatible offer. The accepting party pays gas. Collateral is locked, funds transfer, and both parties receive position NFTs.
howItWorksSection.step3Desc: Monitor the position via the dashboard. Add collateral if LTV rises. Withdraw excess if Health Factor is strong. Get notified before critical thresholds are breached.
howItWorksSection.step4Desc: Repay on time and reclaim the collateral. Lenders claim principal + interest using the NFT. If default occurs, liquidation or full collateral transfer follows the protocol rules.
howItWorksSection.step4Detail3: 0.1% Loan Initiation Fee + 1% Yield Fee on interest
howItWorksSection.step4Detail4: Tiered VPFI discount: 10% / 15% / 20% / 24% off both fees by escrow balance

security.title: Built for safety at every layer
security.subtitle: From smart contract architecture to liquidation mechanics, every design decision prioritizes the safety of assets. Each card below links to the on-chain artifact backing the claim — verify the standard yourself.
security.diamondDesc: Modular proxy architecture. Upgrade individual facets without disrupting the protocol. All calls route through a single diamond entry point.
security.escrowDesc: Each user gets a dedicated UUPS proxy escrow. No commingling of funds. Mandatory escrow upgrades block interactions until the user upgrades.
security.transparencyDesc: Every offer, loan, and status change is recorded on-chain. Vaipakam NFTs serve as verifiable proof of position ownership.
security.slippageDesc: DEX liquidation aborts if slippage exceeds 6%. Falls back to full collateral transfer via NFT claim model. No forced bad swaps.
security.auditDesc: Built on OpenZeppelin contracts. ReentrancyGuard and Pausable on all facets. Mandatory third-party audits before mainnet deployment.
security.kycDesc: Phase 1 launches with KYC checks in pass-through mode. Vaipakam is non-custodial; users are responsible for their own regulatory compliance. Sanctions-country and tiered-KYC logic remains in the codebase for future governance activation.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about lending, borrowing, and building on Vaipakam.
faq.empty: No questions match "{{query}}". Try a different term.

basics-what-is-vaipakam.q: What is Vaipakam, in plain English?
basics-what-is-vaipakam.a1: Vaipakam is a peer-to-peer marketplace for lending crypto and renting digital items (NFTs). One person posts an offer with the terms they're comfortable with — amount, interest, duration — and another person accepts it. No bank, no fund manager, no intermediary sitting between the two counterparties.
basics-what-is-vaipakam.a2: What keeps everyone honest is a set of smart contracts on a blockchain. They hold the assets in individual escrow during the loan and release them automatically when the terms are met. Each user remains in control of their own funds; the protocol simply enforces the deal that was agreed.

basics-wallet.q: Do I need a crypto wallet, and what is one?
basics-wallet.a1: A wallet (MetaMask, Rabby, Coinbase Wallet, and similar) is the app that holds the private key controlling on-chain assets. Think of it as a login and a signing pen rolled into one — it's how Vaipakam knows that a transaction is genuinely coming from the account holder.
basics-wallet.a2: A wallet is only required to act on a position (create an offer, accept a loan, repay, claim). Browsing the analytics dashboard and reading loan details works with no wallet at all. Vaipakam never sees or stores private keys — the wallet lives entirely in the browser or extension.

basics-collateral.a1: Collateral is an asset locked up for the duration of the loan to guarantee repayment. Because the lender does not know the borrower personally, they need a credible commitment that the borrower will not simply walk away with the borrowed funds.
basics-collateral.a2: Every loan on Vaipakam is <s>over-collateralized</s>, meaning the collateral posted is worth more than the amount borrowed. If the loan is repaid as agreed, the borrower gets it all back, no strings attached. If not, the lender has a protocol-enforced path to claim it.

basics-chains.q: Why are there so many different blockchains (Base, Polygon, Arbitrum…)?
basics-chains.a1: A blockchain is the shared ledger that records every transaction. Different blockchains — Ethereum, Base, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism — are independent ledgers, each with its own fees, speed, and set of available tokens. They do not automatically share state.
basics-chains.a2: Vaipakam runs on several of these, and a single loan lives entirely on one chain from start to finish. The chain selector lets the user choose which one to read or transact on. Picking a cheaper L2 like Base or Polygon usually means paying much lower gas fees than Ethereum mainnet.

basics-apr.q: What does "APR" mean on a loan offer?
basics-apr.a1: APR stands for <s>Annual Percentage Rate</s> — the interest rate stated on a yearly basis. A loan with a 10% APR over a full year would cost the borrower 10% of the principal in interest; the same 10% APR over six months costs roughly 5%, and over a month roughly 0.83%.
basics-apr.a2: On the offer screen, the APR and the duration are always shown side by side so the user can sanity-check the total cost before accepting. The exact accrual formula is fixed at the moment the loan starts and cannot be changed afterwards.

basics-position-nft.q: Why was I given an NFT when my loan started?
basics-position-nft.a1: The role in a loan — as lender or borrower — is represented as an NFT instead of an entry in a database. It's essentially a permanent, transferable receipt: it proves which side of the loan the holder is on and is what they present to claim funds later.
basics-position-nft.a2: Because this receipt is an NFT, it is also transferable. Lenders who want to exit early can sell the position to another lender without disturbing the borrower; the loan keeps running unchanged, and the new holder simply takes over the right to be repaid.

basics-gas.q: What are "gas fees", and why does every action cost a little?
basics-gas.a1: Gas is the fee the blockchain itself charges to process a transaction. It pays the network of computers that validate the transaction and add it to the ledger. It is paid in the chain's native token — ETH on Ethereum or Base, MATIC on Polygon, and so on.
basics-gas.a2: Vaipakam never takes a cut of gas — it goes straight to the network. Using an L2 (Base, Arbitrum, Optimism, Polygon) usually keeps gas at a few cents per action, which is why most users prefer them over Ethereum mainnet for day-to-day activity.

basics-custody.q: Who actually holds my money while a loan is active?
basics-custody.a1: Nobody holds it in the traditional sense. Assets live in a smart-contract escrow that only the user and the protocol's own rules can move. Each user has their own isolated escrow — funds are never pooled with strangers — and every release of those funds follows a rule that was published on-chain before the user ever clicked "accept".
basics-custody.a2: The contracts are open source and have been audited. Vaipakam cannot freeze, redirect, or claw back any balance; the most the team can do is pause new actions in an emergency. Repayment, claim, or exit always remains available.

basics-vpfi-intro.q: What is VPFI, and do I need any to use the app?
basics-vpfi-intro.a1: VPFI is Vaipakam's native platform token. It is <s>not</s> required to lend or borrow — the app works entirely without it, and every core feature is open to anyone who shows up.
basics-vpfi-intro.a2: Where VPFI becomes useful is for regular users. Holding it in the Vaipakam escrow grants a discount on platform fees and lets the holder earn passive rewards on top. Think of it as an optional loyalty layer, not a gate.

assets.q: What assets can I lend or borrow?
assets.a1: Vaipakam supports two loan shapes: ERC-20 loans (lend a fungible token) and NFT rentals (rent out an ERC-721 or ERC-1155).
assets.li1: <s>ERC-20 loans:</s> principal is any ERC-20 (USDC, ETH, WBTC, etc.); collateral can be any ERC-20 or any NFT.
assets.li2: <s>NFT rentals:</s> the rented NFT is the principal; collateral is an ERC-20. Native ERC-4907 on the NFT is optional — the per-user escrow wrapper exposes a uniform <c>userOf</c> / <c>userExpires</c> / <c>userQuantity</c> surface regardless.

escrow.q: How does the per-user escrow work?
escrow.a1: Every user gets their own UUPS upgradeable proxy escrow, deployed on first use by the <c>EscrowFactoryFacet</c> (clone-factory pattern). Assets are never commingled with anyone else's.
escrow.a2: The Diamond orchestrates moves across escrows, but claim rights are tied to the Vaipakam position NFT — so escrow custody is strictly constrained by on-chain position state.

default.q: What happens if a borrower defaults?
default.a1: There are two distinct liquidation paths:
default.li1: <s>Health Factor trigger:</s> if HF drops below 1.0 (while the loan is still live), anyone can call the permissionless DEX liquidation. The router swaps collateral via 0x with a 6% max slippage, and the liquidator receives a bonus.
default.li2: <s>Time-based trigger:</s> once the grace period expires, liquid collateral is swapped to repay the lender; illiquid collateral (NFTs, or tokens without a qualifying oracle + v3-style concentrated-liquidity AMM pool) is transferred directly to the lender via the NFT-claim model.
default.a2: If a DEX swap cannot meet the 6% slippage bound, the fallback route delivers collateral-equivalent value to the lender and splits a premium (3% to the lender, 2% to treasury) as a distress bonus.

early-exit.q: Can I exit my position early?
early-exit.a1: Yes — both sides have exits that protect the counterparty's economics:
early-exit.li1: <s>Borrower early repayment:</s> pay principal plus the full term's accrued interest at any time; the lender still receives the yield they were promised.
early-exit.li2: <s>Debt transfer / offset:</s> hand the debt to another borrower, or create an offsetting new position that nets down exposure without touching the lender.
early-exit.li3: <s>Lender early withdrawal / sale:</s> sell the position NFT to another lender, or post an offsetting offer. The underlying loan keeps running unchanged for the borrower.

durations.q: How are loan durations and grace periods determined?
durations.a1: Loan durations are configurable between 1 day and 1 year. Grace periods are derived from the term:

chains.a1: Phase 1 targets <s>Base</s>, <s>Polygon</s>, <s>Arbitrum</s>, <s>Optimism</s>, and <s>Ethereum mainnet</s>. Base is the canonical chain for VPFI (where the token is minted and the fixed-price buy lives), but the in-app Buy VPFI page works from every supported chain — it auto-selects a direct buy on Base, or a LayerZero-bridged buy from any mirror chain, so the user never has to switch networks just to top up.
chains.a2: The live deployment today is <s>Sepolia</s> while additional chains are being rolled out. All aspects of a single loan must occur on the same network.

sequencer.q: What if the L2 sequencer goes down?
sequencer.a1: On supported L2s, the protocol reads the Chainlink sequencer uptime feed. If the sequencer is reported down, or has been back up for less than a 1-hour grace window, oracle-sensitive actions — new loans, HF-based liquidations, and rate-dependent quotes — fail-closed. Repayments and user-initiated exits keep working so a user can never be trapped in a position.

liquidity-status.q: How does Vaipakam decide if an asset is liquid or illiquid?
liquidity-status.a1: Liquidity is judged <s>only on the network being transacted on</s>. The protocol checks two things on that chain: a reliable Chainlink price path for the asset, and a sufficiently deep v3-style concentrated-liquidity AMM <c>asset/WETH</c> 0.3% pool. If either fails, the asset is marked <s2>illiquid on that network</s2>.
liquidity-status.a2: There is no Ethereum-mainnet fallback. An asset that's liquid on mainnet but not on, say, Base is simply illiquid on Base — the protocol will not reach back to mainnet to justify liquid treatment. If the asset is illiquid, LTV / Health Factor checks are skipped (the collateral is valued at $0), and both parties must accept the combined abnormal-market + illiquid-assets risk acknowledgement before the offer can proceed.

preclose-refinance.q: What are preclose, refinance, and lender early-withdrawal?
preclose-refinance.a1: Beyond regular repayment, there are three strategic-exit flows:
preclose-refinance.li1: <s>Borrower preclose:</s> the borrower settles the loan ahead of schedule. While the preclose flow is in progress, the borrower-side position NFT is <s2>locked for transfer</s2> until the flow completes, is cancelled, or is unwound — a notice about this appears in the transaction review before confirmation.
preclose-refinance.li2: <s>Lender early-withdrawal:</s> the lender exits the loan before its natural end. Same transfer-lock rules apply to the lender-side NFT for the duration of the flow.
preclose-refinance.li3: <s>Refinance:</s> the borrower swaps one active loan for a fresh loan with a new lender. By the time the borrower signs the refinance transaction, the replacement lender has already accepted the new offer and the replacement loan already exists — the on-chain refinance call is a single atomic settlement, so there is no refinance-specific NFT lock to worry about.

keepers.q: What are keepers, and can they take my assets?
keepers.a1: Keepers are optional <s>delegated role-managers</s> — they can execute the on-chain role assigned to them (for example, calling <c>repayLoan</c> on the user's behalf or triggering a default settlement), but they <s2>cannot claim assets</s2>. Claims are always locked to whoever currently holds the relevant Vaipakam position NFT, which is the user unless they have transferred it.
keepers.a2: Enabling a keeper takes <s>two opt-ins</s>, by design:
keepers.li1: <s>User-level:</s> in advanced settings, the user must turn on keeper access for their side (borrower or lender) and add the keeper to a whitelist.
keepers.li2: <s>Position-level:</s> the offer (or accept) flow must have keeper execution enabled for that specific position.
keepers.a3: If either layer is off, the keeper cannot act — flipping the position-level flag alone is not enough. Keeper access for an existing loan can also be enabled or disabled later, from advanced settings, for the user's own side. Keeper controls only appear in <s>Advanced mode</s>; they are intentionally hidden from the default everyday workflow.

fees.q: What fees does the platform charge?
fees.a1: Two headline fees, plus a late-payment schedule:
fees.li1: <s>Loan Initiation Fee — 0.1%</s> on ERC-20 loans, deducted from the lender's principal at loan start. The borrower receives 99.9% of the offered amount but still owes the full principal.
fees.li2: <s>Yield Fee — 1%</s> on interest earned by lenders and on rental income earned by NFT owners.
fees.li3: <s>Late fees:</s> 1% flat on the day of miss, plus 0.5% per additional day, capped at 5% of principal. Late fees route to treasury.
fees.a2: VPFI holders can reduce both headline fees via a single platform-level consent — see the VPFI discount entry below.

vpfi-discount.q: How does the VPFI discount work?
vpfi-discount.a1: VPFI held in the Vaipakam escrow automatically places the holder in a discount tier. One platform-level consent covers both the borrower (initiation) and lender (yield) legs, and the discounted fee is auto-deducted in VPFI from the escrow — the borrower then receives the full principal and the lender keeps the full interest on the discount path.

vpfi-rewards.q: How can I earn VPFI rewards?
vpfi-rewards.a1: Two reward surfaces, both keyed to the escrow balance — no extra staking contract to manage:
vpfi-rewards.li1: <s>Staking rewards — 5% APR</s> on VPFI held in escrow (24% of total supply = 55.2M VPFI allocated to this pool). The balance accrues continuously while it sits there.
vpfi-rewards.li2: <s>Interaction rewards</s> pay VPFI on loan interest paid or received, capped at <s2>0.5 VPFI per 0.001 ETH of interest</s2> per user, drawn from the 30% interaction-rewards pool (69M VPFI). The cap prevents wash-loan farming.

tokenomics.a1: Hard cap of <s>230M VPFI</s>. Genesis mint is <s2>23M (10%)</s2> on Base; the remaining supply unlocks against usage and pool drawdowns. Fixed-rate purchase is pinned at <s3>1 VPFI = 0.001 ETH</s3>.
tokenomics.a2: Early purchase is capped at 2.3M VPFI (1% of supply), with a per-wallet per-chain cap of 30,000 VPFI during the bootstrap window. Allocations:

nft-positions.a1: Every offer and every loan mints a unique ERC-721 with on-chain metadata (asset types, amounts, rates, status). The NFT is the position — it proves ownership and is required to claim funds after repayment, default, or liquidation. Because metadata is on-chain, any standard NFT marketplace can render the current state.

integrator-rental.q: How do I read rental state for a Vaipakam-rented NFT?
integrator-rental.a1: Query the lender's Vaipakam escrow wrapper — not the underlying NFT — through the Diamond. For ERC-1155 rentals with multiple concurrent renters on the same (nftContract, tokenId), escrowGetNFTQuantity returns the active aggregate quantity and escrowGetNFTUserExpires returns the minimum active expiry.
integrator-rental.a2: The wrapper maintains authoritative rental state even when the underlying NFT does <s>not</s> implement ERC-4907, so integrators get a uniform surface across every rented asset.

integrator-subgraph.q: What loan-lifecycle events can I index?
integrator-subgraph.a1: The Diamond emits a stable set of lifecycle events that the reference subgraph indexes. Subscribe to these directly, or query the subgraph:
integrator-subgraph.li3: <c>LoanDefaulted</c> — grace period elapsed, claim path unlocked
integrator-subgraph.li5: <c>LiquidationFallback</c> — DEX path blocked; collateral-equivalent + premium paid out
